description: Export local SeeFlow diagrams to seeflow.dev and share via link. Anyone with the link can view the diagram read-only.

userStories.title: CDK project scaffold
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the apps/cloud/ CDK project initialised so subsequent stories can add resources to it.
acceptanceCriteria: apps/cloud/package.json exists with aws-cdk-lib and constructs dependencies
apps/cloud/cdk.json exists with app entry pointing to bin/app.ts
apps/cloud/bin/app.ts creates a CDK App and instantiates SeeflowStack
apps/cloud/lib/seeflow-stack.ts exports SeeflowStack (empty stack is fine)
apps/cloud/lambda/api/index.ts exists with a stub handler exporting handler function
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: S3 buckets for diagrams and static viewer
userStories.description: As a developer, I need two S3 buckets defined in SeeflowStack: one private bucket for diagram data and one for the static viewer SPA.
acceptanceCriteria: SeeflowStack defines a private S3 Bucket named 'seeflow-diagrams' (versioned, RETAIN removal policy)
SeeflowStack defines a second S3 Bucket for the static viewer SPA (public read blocked, accessed via CloudFront OAC only)
Both bucket logical IDs are exported as stack outputs
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Lambda function and API Gateway HTTP API
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a Lambda function and API Gateway HTTP API wired together so the API routes are reachable.
acceptanceCriteria: SeeflowStack defines a NodejsFunction (or equivalent) pointing to apps/cloud/lambda/api/index.ts
Lambda has read/write IAM permissions on the diagrams S3 bucket
SeeflowStack defines an HttpApi with routes: POST /flows, GET /flows/{uuid}, GET /flows/{uuid}/files/{proxy+}
All routes integrate with the Lambda function
API Gateway endpoint URL exported as stack output
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: POST /flows Lambda handler — accept zip, store in S3
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the POST /flows handler to accept a zip upload, unzip it in memory, and write the contents to the diagrams S3 bucket.
acceptanceCriteria: Handler reads email from query string parameter 'email'
Handler reads zip bytes from the request body (base64-decoded if needed for API Gateway proxy)
Rejects requests where total zip size exceeds 50MB or any single file exceeds 10MB
Generates a UUID using crypto.randomUUID()
Unzips in memory and writes each entry to s3://diagrams-bucket/{uuid}/{entry-path}
Writes s3://diagrams-bucket/{uuid}/metadata.json containing { email, createdAt: ISO string }
Returns JSON { url: 'https://seeflow.dev/flow/{uuid}' } with status 201
Returns 400 for missing email, 413 for oversized upload
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: GET /flows/{uuid} and GET /flows/{uuid}/files/{proxy+} Lambda handlers
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the read endpoints so the viewer can fetch seeflow.json and bundled asset files.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /flows/{uuid} reads s3://diagrams-bucket/{uuid}/seeflow.json and returns it as application/json
Returns 404 JSON { error: 'Not found' } if the key does not exist in S3
GET /flows/{uuid}/files/{proxy+} streams s3://diagrams-bucket/{uuid}/files/{proxy} with inferred Content-Type
Content-Type is inferred from file extension (png→image/png, jpg→image/jpeg, html→text/html, svg→image/svg+xml, default→application/octet-stream)
Returns 404 if the file key does not exist in S3
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: CloudFront distribution, ACM certificate, and Route53 A record
userStories.description: As a developer, I need CloudFront wired to the domain seeflow.dev so the viewer SPA and API are reachable at the public URL.
acceptanceCriteria: ACM certificate for seeflow.dev is requested in us-east-1 with DNS validation against the existing Route53 hosted zone
CloudFront Distribution has two origins: S3 static bucket (via OAC) and API Gateway HTTP API
Behaviour /api/* routes to the API Gateway origin
Default behaviour /* routes to the S3 static origin with SPA fallback (404 → index.html, 200)
Distribution uses the ACM certificate and CNAME seeflow.dev
Route53 A record (alias) for seeflow.dev points to the CloudFront distribution
CloudFront distribution domain exported as stack output
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: CDK deploy and smoke test of live API
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to deploy the CDK stack and verify the API endpoints are reachable at seeflow.dev.
acceptanceCriteria: Run 'cdk deploy' from apps/cloud/ without errors
POST https://seeflow.dev/api/flows?email=test@test.com with a valid zip returns 201 and a url field
GET https://seeflow.dev/api/flows/{uuid} returns the seeflow.json from the uploaded zip
Stack outputs (bucket names, API URL, CloudFront domain) are visible in AWS console

userStories.title: Viewer app project scaffold
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the apps/viewer/ Vite + React project initialised with routing so the /flow/:uuid page can be built.
acceptanceCriteria: apps/viewer/package.json exists with react, react-dom, react-router-dom, @xyflow/react, vite dependencies
apps/viewer/vite.config.ts configures the build output to apps/viewer/dist/
apps/viewer/src/main.tsx mounts the app with a BrowserRouter
Route /flow/:uuid renders a placeholder FlowView component
Default route / renders a simple 'SeeFlow' page
bun run build in apps/viewer/ produces dist/ without errors
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Viewer flow-view.tsx — fetch and display seeflow.json
userStories.description: As a viewer, I want the /flow/:uuid page to load the diagram data from the API so the canvas can be rendered.
acceptanceCriteria: flow-view.tsx extracts uuid from useParams()
On mount fetches GET https://seeflow.dev/api/flows/{uuid}
Shows a centred spinner while loading
Shows a centred error message if the fetch fails or returns 404
On success passes the parsed Demo object to ViewCanvas
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Viewer view-canvas.tsx — read-only React Flow canvas
userStories.description: As a viewer, I want to see the diagram rendered as a React Flow canvas with no interactive controls.
acceptanceCriteria: view-canvas.tsx renders a ReactFlow component with nodes and edges derived from the Demo object
Node types used: all node types from apps/web (playNode, stateNode, shapeNode, imageNode, iconNode, group, htmlNode) imported or re-implemented
nodesDraggable={false}, nodesConnectable={false}, elementsSelectable={false}
panOnDrag={true} so users can pan the canvas
No MiniMap, no Controls toolbar, no Background grid
Canvas fills the full viewport
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill — navigate to a test UUID and confirm the canvas renders

userStories.title: Viewer imageNode and htmlNode cloud URL rewriting
userStories.description: As a viewer, I want imageNode and htmlNode assets to load from the API rather than a local studio path.
acceptanceCriteria: view-canvas.tsx accepts a uuid prop and passes it as context/prop to node renderers
imageNode renderer builds src as https://seeflow.dev/api/flows/{uuid}/files/{data.path}
htmlNode renderer fetches content from https://seeflow.dev/api/flows/{uuid}/files/{data.htmlPath}
Nodes with missing files show a placeholder card (same as local studio behaviour)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Deploy viewer to S3 via CDK BucketDeployment
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the viewer SPA build output deployed to the static S3 bucket so seeflow.dev serves the app.
acceptanceCriteria: SeeflowStack includes a BucketDeployment that uploads apps/viewer/dist/ to the static viewer bucket
BucketDeployment invalidates the CloudFront distribution after each deploy
Running cdk deploy after building apps/viewer/ serves the viewer at https://seeflow.dev
Navigating to https://seeflow.dev/flow/any-uuid loads the viewer page without a 403 or 404
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: useExportToCloud hook
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a hook that bundles the current demo into a zip and uploads it to the cloud API.
acceptanceCriteria: apps/web/src/hooks/use-export-to-cloud.ts exports useExportToCloud(projectId: string)
Hook reads the current demo JSON from the local studio API (GET /api/projects/:id or equivalent)
Hook collects imageNode .data.path and htmlNode .data.htmlPath values from the demo nodes
Hook fetches each referenced file from GET /api/projects/:id/files/:path
Hook zips seeflow.json + files/ using fflate (zipSync or zip) in memory — no temp files
Hook POSTs the zip to https://seeflow.dev/api/flows?email=<email> with Content-Type: application/zip
Hook returns { shareUrl: string } on success or throws on error
fflate added to apps/web package.json dependencies
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: ExportDialog component
userStories.description: As a user, I want a dialog that collects my email, uploads the diagram, and shows me the share link.
acceptanceCriteria: apps/web/src/components/export-dialog.tsx exports ExportDialog
Dialog accepts props: open, onOpenChange, projectId
On open, pre-fills email input from localStorage key 'seeflow.export.email'
idle state: email input + 'Export' button
loading state: spinner + 'Uploading…' text, inputs disabled
done state: share URL in a read-only input + 'Copy link' button + 'Done' button
error state: error message + 'Try again' button that returns to idle
On successful export: saves email to localStorage 'seeflow.export.email'
Copy link button copies the URL to clipboard
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill — open the dialog, enter email, confirm states render correctly

userStories.title: Wire ExportDialog into ShareMenu and demo-view
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see 'Export to seeflow.dev' in the Share menu so I can trigger the export flow.
acceptanceCriteria: ShareMenu accepts new optional prop onExportToCloud: () => void
When onExportToCloud is provided, a third menu item 'Export to seeflow.dev' appears in the dropdown with an Upload icon
Clicking 'Export to seeflow.dev' calls onExportToCloud
demo-view.tsx renders ExportDialog and passes open/onOpenChange state
demo-view.tsx passes onExportToCloud to ShareMenu that sets exportDialogOpen to true
ShareMenu tests updated to cover the new prop
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill — open the Share menu on a loaded demo and confirm 'Export to seeflow.dev' is visible

userStories.title: End-to-end verification on seeflow.dev
userStories.description: As a user, I want to export a real diagram from the local studio and verify the share link renders correctly on seeflow.dev.
acceptanceCriteria: Local studio is running with the order-pipeline demo loaded
User opens Share menu, clicks 'Export to seeflow.dev', enters email, hits Export
ExportDialog transitions to done state and shows a share URL
Navigating to the share URL in a browser shows the read-only canvas with all nodes visible
Canvas can be panned but nodes cannot be dragged or selected
No toolbar, no sidebar, no play buttons visible on the viewer page
imageNode and htmlNode assets (if present in the demo) render correctly from the cloud URL
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill — navigate to the generated seeflow.dev/flow/{uuid} URL and confirm the above
